name: end_allows_get
reason: If provable, end() produces an iterator that is NOT considered "end", contradicting its purpose as a sentinel

name: is_end_always_true
reason: If provable, is_end returns true for every iterator, making it impossible to distinguish live iterators from the end sentinel

name: get_collapses_keys
code:
proof fn phi_3_get_collapses_keys<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(a: KeyIterator<K>, b: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        !a.is_end_spec(),
        !b.is_end_spec(),
    ensures
        a.get_spec() == b.get_spec(),
{
}
reason: If provable, get returns the same key for all non-end iterators, meaning the spec fails to distinguish different iterator positions

name: end_has_some_key
reason: If provable, end() yields an iterator carrying a key despite its spec promising k.is_None(), indicating an inconsistent postcondition

name: no_non_end_exists
reason: If provable, every iterator is end so get's precondition is unsatisfiable, making get vacuously correct but useless
